title: DSDS Token and Token Group Definitions
description: Documentation for individual design tokens and hierarchical token groups. Token values, aliases, and type information live in the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) file — DSDS documents the purpose, guidelines, and organizational structure only. Token groups are flexible organizational units — a full token collection, a token family (e.g., all color tokens), or a sub-family (e.g., a single hue family). Groups can contain tokens, nested groups, or both, forming a hierarchy that mirrors how design systems organize tokens. The displayName property is omitted from tokens and token groups — the token name itself serves as the display name. Description and status are optional to reduce verbosity at scale. When omitted on children within a token group, status is inherited from the parent group.

token.description: Documentation for a single design token — name, type, use cases, guidelines, and accessibility considerations. The displayName property is absent — the token name is the display name. Description and status are optional. When a token appears inside a token group, an omitted status is inherited from the parent group.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token.

name.description: The token name. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions that use dots, slashes, or other separators (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary'). Also serves as the human-readable display name.

extends.description: Declares that this token inherits from a base token in a parent design system. The base token provides the foundational type, source reference, and guidelines. This token layers on top — providing additional guidelines or context while inheriting everything else.

aliases.description: Alternative names this token is known by across teams, tools, or legacy systems. Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing.

summary.description: A one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup.

thumbnail.description: A visual for compact display contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards.

description.description: A description of the token — what it represents, where to use it, and any constraints. Optional; when omitted, tools MAY generate a description from the token name and type.

preview.description: A visual or interactive preview — a hero image, video walkthrough, code snippet, or link to a live demo. Accepts any presentation type from the presentation schema.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the token. Optional. When omitted inside a token group, inherited from the parent group. When omitted at the top level, tools SHOULD treat the token as having no declared status.

since.description: The version in which this token was introduced.

tags.description: Freeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories/disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation').

tokenType.description: The type of token aligned with the DTCG spec (e.g., 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'cubicBezier', 'shadow').

category.description: The token's semantic category. Standard values: 'base' (raw palette values, not used directly in components), 'semantic' (purpose-driven tokens that map to base values, e.g., 'color-text-primary'), 'component' (tokens scoped to a specific component, e.g., 'button-background'). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case.

source.description: Reference to the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this token's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this token within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text.primary').

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Tokens accept general document block types only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Each document block entry has a `type` property that determines its shape. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

links.description: External resource links, related artifact references, and internal relationships. Each link can point to an external URL, an internal DSDS artifact by name, or both.

tokenGroup.description: A group of related design tokens. Token groups are flexible organizational units — a full collection, a family (e.g., all color tokens), a sub-family (e.g., a single hue with its grade scale), or any other logical grouping. Groups can nest to form a deep hierarchy. A top-level group might represent 'color', containing child groups for 'color-text', 'color-background', and 'color-border', each with individual tokens. This recursive structure mirrors how real design systems organize tokens into families and sub-families. The displayName property is absent — the group name is the display name. Description and status are optional. Children inherit the parent group's status when they omit their own.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token-group.

name.description: The token group name. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text', 'color.text', 'color/text'). Also serves as the human-readable display name.

aliases.description: Alternative names this token group is known by across teams, tools, or legacy systems. Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing.

summary.description: A one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup.

thumbnail.description: A visual for compact display contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards.

description.description: A description of the group's purpose, the tokens it contains, and how they relate. Optional; when omitted, tools MAY generate a description from the group name and children.

preview.description: A visual or interactive preview — a hero image, video walkthrough, code snippet, or link to a live demo. Accepts any presentation type from the presentation schema.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the token group. Optional. When omitted inside a parent token group, inherited from the parent. Children that omit their own status inherit this group's status.

since.description: The version in which this group was introduced.

tags.description: Freeform keywords for categorization and search (e.g., 'color', 'palette', 'semantic').

tokenType.description: When all tokens in this group share the same DTCG token type, declare it here to avoid repetition on each child. Each token in children MAY override this value. Common values: 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'number', 'shadow'.

category.description: When all tokens in this group share the same semantic category, declare it here. Standard values: 'base', 'semantic', 'component'. Each token in children MAY override this value.

source.description: Reference to the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this group's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this group within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text').

children.description: An ordered array of tokens and/or nested token groups in this group. Each item MUST be either a token or a tokenGroup. Ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve order for display, as it often represents a meaningful progression (e.g., lightest to darkest, or smallest to largest). Children inherit tokenType, category, and status from this group when they omit their own values.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token group as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts general document block types only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Document blocks for the group as a whole live here. Document blocks for individual tokens SHOULD be placed on the token within children.

links.description: External resource links, related artifact references, and internal relationships.
